nodes.title: Resolve run-scoped workflow artifacts
acceptanceCriteria: Expand {runId} in required outputs to the current story run directory.
Validate the expanded run-scoped task DAG path.
Keep workflows without placeholders and all M2 regression tests compatible.

nodes.title: Prepare and inspect structured dispatch tasks
acceptanceCriteria: prepare writes task.json with owner, revision, expected outputs, and allowed adapters.
Repeated prepare reuses the dispatchId and status remains read-only.
Blocked, completed, or identity-mismatched runs do not create tasks.

nodes.title: Run fixed command adapters and apply results
acceptanceCriteria: run-adapter accepts fixed IDs and argv only and writes structured evidence.
apply validates story, phase, dispatchId, output set, and run directory boundary.
completed, failed, and blocked results advance, stay, or call M2 block respectively, with resumable partial apply.
Failed tests and unresolved BLOCKER findings cannot pass M2 gates.

nodes.title: Verify the complete vertical slice and Harness integration
acceptanceCriteria: A fixture story reaches delivery summary and completes when approval evidence exists.
Tests cover missing artifacts, failed adapters, BLOCKER findings, and restart recovery.
M2 and M3 tests, structure, state, DAG, smoke, and diff checks pass.
Documentation distinguishes implemented M3 behavior from M4 and later scope.

edges.reason: Dispatch tasks must reference resolved run-scoped required outputs.
Result application and adapters reuse the task and checkpoint contract.
The vertical test requires completed dispatch and apply behavior.

globalChanges: Switch e2e workflow required outputs to run-scoped placeholders while preserving M2 compatibility.
Register the M3 runtime, schemas, tests, and docs in the Harness structure manifest.
risks: T1 through T3 share runtime files and must execute serially with targeted M2 and M3 regression checks.
The active M3 bootstrap state uses legacy global outputs and must enter implementation before the workflow switches paths.
